definition: in large quantities; plentifully
The evidence was abundantly clear.
in a way that is appropriate
The weather forecast was bad, and we dressed accordingly.
in an administrative manner
The new policy was implemented administratively without a vote.
In conflict or difficulty with
He ran afoul of the law.
allegedly
He allegedly committed the crime.
up in the air; overhead
The flag waved proudly aloft.
with reference to; concerning (often used to introduce a relevant topic)
Apropos of your earlier comment, I've found some new information.
certainly; without a doubt
He assuredly knew the answer to the question.
into pieces
The old book fell asunder in his hands.
in a biological way; from a biological point of view.
Biologically, humans are very similar to other primates.
absolutely; without qualification
He categorically denied the accusations.
In a way that relates to cognition; mentally.
The patient's ability to process information cognitively was impaired.
In a way that relates to or involves computation.
The problem is computationally intensive, requiring powerful computers.
In terms of concepts or ideas.
Conceptually, the plan was sound, but practical implementation was difficult.
in a way that is allowed or restricted by a constitution
The new law was deemed constitutionally sound.
in a way that can be clearly shown or proved
The new policy was demonstrably effective in reducing crime.
completely; directly opposite
Their opinions were diametrically opposed on the issue.
to an extent that is too large or too small in comparison with something else
The rich disproportionately benefit from these tax cuts.
in accordance with what is required or appropriate; properly
The documents were duly signed and submitted.
by means of observation or experience rather than theory or pure logic
The theory was empirically tested through experiments.
in an unpredictable or irregular way
The car was swerving erratically down the road.
in a mistaken or incorrect way
He erroneously believed the train would arrive on time.
forever; always
She vowed to love him evermore.
to an unusually great degree; extremely
The task was exceedingly difficult.
in a clear and detailed manner, leaving no room for confusion or doubt
The rules were explicitly stated in the contract.
for a particular purpose; explicitly
The rules were expressly designed to prevent cheating.
beautifully
The painting was exquisitely detailed.
done by force
The police made a forcible entry into the building.
immediately; without delay
The order must be carried out forthwith.
given or done unwillingly (adjective)
He gave a grudging apology, clearly not meaning it.
with the head foremost; in a rush
He rushed headlong into the crowd.
from this time forward
Henceforth, all employees must wear their ID badges.
from this time on or in the future; in the afterlife
Hereafter, all meetings will begin promptly at 9 AM.
by this means; as a result of this
I hereby declare the meeting open.
until now or until the point in time under discussion
Hitherto, the project had been running smoothly.
in a way that relates to or is based on ideology
The two parties were ideologically opposed on many issues.
not resulting from or achieved through deliberate planning
His inadvertent remark caused some offense.
in a way that cannot be challenged or denied
He is indisputably the best player on the team.
impossible to stop or prevent
The inexorable march of time affects us all.
in a way that cannot be explained
The lights went out inexplicably.
in a way that is impossible to disentangle or separate
Their fates were inextricably linked.
to the extent that
Insofar as I understand the situation, we are making good progress.
at once; instantly
The light came on instantaneously when she flipped the switch.
based on instinct, automatic
His reaction was purely instinctive.
in a way that can be exchanged without making a difference
The terms 'happy' and 'joyful' are often used interchangeably.
never changing; constant
His invariable good humor was a comfort to everyone.
not able to be changed, reversed, or recovered
The decision was irrevocable.
recently; in latter times
Latterly, he has shown great improvement in his studies.
noticeably
Her performance has improved markedly since last year.
in a very skillful or expert way
The artist masterfully blended the colors in the painting.
Significantly; substantially
The new policy will materially affect our profits.
in a morbid manner
He was morbidly fascinated by true crime stories.
not a single; no
There was nary a sound in the empty house.
in name only; theoretically
He is nominally in charge, but others make the decisions.
in spite of that; nevertheless
It was a difficult task, but he succeeded nonetheless.
in spite of; nevertheless
Notwithstanding the bad weather, the event was a success.
not in a direct way; indirectly
He obliquely hinted at the problem without directly addressing it.
as appears or is stated to be true, though not necessarily so
He resigned ostensibly due to health reasons, but many suspected otherwise.
openly, not secretly
He overtly expressed his disapproval of the plan.
with great care and thoroughness
He painstakingly restored the antique clock to its original condition.
Clearly; obviously.
His excuse was patently false; no one believed him.
continuously, endlessly
The old clock in the hall perpetually chimed every hour.
exceptionally, remarkably
The new product sold phenomenally well in its first month.
in a physiological manner
The body reacts physiologically to stress by releasing hormones.
in a way that seems reasonable or probable
He argued his case quite plausibly, despite the lack of concrete evidence.
in a way that shows preference
We will preferentially select candidates with relevant experience.
abundant; flowing freely
He offered his profuse apologies for the mistake.
to an extent that prevents something from being done or achieved
The cost of the new software was prohibitively expensive for small businesses.
in a psychological manner; relating to the mind
The experience affected him psychologically.
appearing or stated to be true, though not necessarily so; allegedly
The purported leader of the group denied the accusations.
in terms of quality or kind rather than quantity
The new system is qualitatively better than the old one.
according to what is generally said or believed
He is reputedly the best chef in the city.
with effect from a date in the past
The new policy will apply retroactively to the beginning of the year.
in a thorough or emphatic manner
He was roundly criticized for his actions.
without pity or compassion
He ruthlessly pursued his goals.
in a scanty or meager way; barely
She was scantily dressed for the cold weather.
in a singular or unique way; exceptionally
She was singularly talented among her peers.
in a severe or bare manner
The truth was starkly revealed in the report.
In a way that is very noticeable or impressive.
Her new painting was strikingly beautiful.
in an extremely impressive or attractive manner
The view from the mountain was stunningly beautiful.
without conscious awareness
He subconsciously tapped his foot during the meeting.
to a great extent
The cost has increased substantially.
in a summary manner; without delay or formality
The employee was summarily dismissed for misconduct.
kept secret, especially because it would not be approved of
They had a surreptitious meeting in the park.
after that
He moved to London in 2005 and lived there thereafter.
by that means
He failed the exam, thereby losing his scholarship.
in that place, document, or respect
The contract outlines the terms, and therein lies the problem.
three times
He knocked thrice before entering the room.
in a way that shows no regret or apology
She stated her opinion unapologetically.
without the knowledge of (someone)
Unbeknownst to him, his friends were planning a surprise party.
without conditions or limitations
She loved him unconditionally.
in a way that cannot be denied
He is undeniably one of the best players in the league.
to an unwarranted or excessive degree
Don't be unduly concerned about the minor delay.
in a way that leaves no doubt; unambiguously.
She stated unequivocally that she would not support the proposal.
done by one person or group without the agreement of others
The company decided to unilaterally change the terms of the contract.
beyond doubt
She was unquestionably the best candidate for the job.
without being aware; unintentionally
She unwittingly revealed the secret during the conversation.
in an upward direction or position
The company is moving upwardly in the market.
completely and without qualification
She was utterly exhausted after the long journey.
with courage or determination
The small army fought valiantly against overwhelming odds.
in different ways or at different times
The problem has been variously described as a crisis or a challenge.
in exactly the same words as were used originally
She repeated his instructions verbatim to ensure accuracy.
experienced in the imagination through the feelings or actions of another person
She felt a vicarious thrill watching the extreme sports.
in what place or respect; in which
The contract specifies the terms wherein the agreement is valid.
entirely; fully
The decision was wholly unexpected.
full of woe; sorrowful
His woeful expression showed how disappointed he was.
at some distance in the direction indicated
Look at the beautiful mountains over yonder.
